scenarios.name: Bull
scenarios.description: MI450 on-time H2 2026, mega-deals at 95% realization, ROCm closes CUDA gap for inference, 60% GM, EPYC 50%+ share, AI capex cycle extends 3+ years

scenarios.name: Base
scenarios.description: MI450 minor delay (1-2 quarters), mega-deals at 65% realization, ROCm adequate for inference workloads, 57.5% GM, ASIC competition limits GPU share gains

scenarios.name: Bear
scenarios.description: MI450 delayed to 2027, mega-deals prove to be bridge contracts before ASIC transition, 40% realization, AI capex cycle corrects within 12 months, CUDA moat unbreached

kelly_recommendation: Do not initiate new position — negative expected value at current price
conviction_rationale: Low conviction due to: 4 unresolved contradictions between analysts, 55-65% data completeness, bear probability (30%) near break-even threshold (33-36%), composite thesis fragility 3.8/5
key_swing_factors: MI450 production timing (H2 2026 vs 2027 delay) — binary event worth $60-80/share
Mega-deal realization rate (40-95% range shifts price by ~$120)
WACC/Beta assumption (14-18% range shifts price by $60-80)
Bridge contract hypothesis — if mega-deals are temporary, bull case collapses
Custom ASIC substitution rate (OpenAI Broadcom deal 67% larger than AMD commitment)
key_unresolved_risks: Bridge contract vs permanent GPU adoption — no resolution between Devil's Advocate and Sector/Catalyst
AI capex cycle duration — Macro says 6-18 months remaining vs Sector says 3-4 years
ROIC vs WACC — GAAP ROIC 6.6% below any WACC estimate implies value destruction
Fair value range spans $120-250 across analyst methodologies
